item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the wholesomeness and overall making sense (at least imo)
i was a little confused at first with the notion of depression, but animals can actually experience something similar, so it shouldn't really be this much of a surprise (thanks biologist cousin!). it aint perfect, but it doesn't have to be. the informalities don't come off as unnecessary or coming out of nowhere - it makes sense that the people caring for that komodo enough to discuss how to help him out of his loss of apetite would face difficulties at sounding unbiased in the documentation of their course of action. well done buddy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall a cool idea but I think the Writing is off. somethings come off as informal or confusing.
Here are some points

+ show block
– hide block

SCP-4191 is capable of generating fire through SCP-4191-ẟ by self-immolation

This sentence is confusing, does 4191 or 4191-ẟ produce the fire? it seems to say it's the costume but "self-immolation" would mean it's the lizard.

is a Komodo Dragon (Varanus komodoensis) suffering from pyromania

as others pointed out this is not possible, (unless it is sapient witch is never stated) using something like its behavior is similar to pyromania in humans makes more sense.

has been affectionately nicknamed by the staff of Site ██ assigned to it as "Pyro".

considering the footnote states its against protocol why would the official document include this if its not pertinent to the item?

using the term "light up" seem informal, I would try something like "ignite"

lastly it is abnormally friendly for a komodo dragon and this is never mentioned in the article.

I hope this helps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps I don't read enough of the articles around here but I rarely get to see SCP articles interacting with the other national branches for positive or negative effects so that was nice. Also like the more unprofessional way the researchers are communicating with each other feels more human and up to date with the current times, though I'm certain some people will disagree with that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First of all, I like this enough to give it a +1.

However, as far as criticism goes, I will concur with Plilt that keeping it in a metal box is maybe not the best call on behalf of the foundation. I could see an unsympathetic containment lead making that decision, but it feels like the Ethics department might have something to say about it, even if it's probably not at the top of their list.

Likewise, I do agree that introducing a non-anomalous Komodo might not be the best call on their part. Again, not entirely unbelievable, mistakes happen, but, at least in my headcanon, I imagine Ethics might get involved and give a reprimand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's true, but they also started by trying to introduce a non-anomalous Komodo dragon, who is definitely going to act like a Komodo dragon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You must understand that while yes 4191 and 124-FR to an extent ressembles Komodo dragons. They aren't 100% especially considering the fact that 124-FR just looks like Komodo dragons and where found in Australia, not the Komodo islands. But it is a valid criticism and it's unfortunate you didn't like the article, you can't please everyone after all ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cute, but the Komodo dragons are not acting like Komodo dragons, at all. They're acting like dogs. Yes, most of them are anomalous, but it doesn't makes sense that they'd immediately jump to "Oh, they're lonely" rather than "Maybe keeping them in a bare metal cell is bad for them."

-1 for poor animal behavior.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, that shows me. Changing it to a novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a Komodo dragon just in case (it is stated in the article after all) the Komodo and the costume can be taken apart from one another but they need to be together to create flames. As for the sapience it was my first time dealing with it on a big scale so i probably need more training using them.

Also i don't get the -1 because of the mention of Titanium, its a really heat-resistant metal why is that a bad thing ? I didn't add it in the article because "duh titanium stronk", I had a legitimate reason to add it haha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the iguana sapient? Because I'm pretty sure animals can't be diagnosed with pyromania. Additionally if the costume is seperate and the iguana is not sapient, why is it here? Does it only work with the one iguana?

-1 for the sapience confusion, and the pointless mention of titanium.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Decided to try my hand at a wholesome entry, I hope you will all like it !

Source of the original image:
https://www.needpix.com/photo/616360/komodo-dragon-island-komodo-tongue-scavengers-monitor-giant-lizard-dangerous-endangered-carnivorous-dinosaurs
